title: erica abietina subsp. diabolis
intro: Erica abietina subsp. diabolis, also known as the Mountain Heath or Diabolis Heath, is a rare and endemic subspecies of plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the mountains of the western Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Erica abietina subsp. diabolis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
